title: FixNCut v2.0 Enhanced Cell and Tissue Fixation for Single Cell Assays
author: Shuoshuo Wang

steps_raw.instruction: Mix 12.5 µL of 50x DSP ( 50 mg/mL ) with 587.5 µL of pre-cooled cold methanol at 4 °C in a 1.5 or 2mL tube to achieve a total volume of 600 µL and final DSP concentration of 1 mg/mL . Each tube can be used for one samples with up to 4 × 10 6 cells. Compared to aqueous condition, methanol offers a controlled medium for DSP dissolution and reactions, reducing NHS ester hydrolysis, especially in its anhydrous form. However, DSP solutions in methanol should still be freshly prepared for best results. Avoid making large master mix for multiple samples, instead, prepare multiple aliquots in parallel.

Gradually add the DSP-methanol fixative to cells and gently resuspend multiple times with a wide-bore pipette, or to tissue samples (cut into 3x3x3 mm pieces or smaller). Swirl gently to ensure mixing.

Fix On ice for 00:30:00 , swirling occasionally. If necessary, increase the fixation time incrementally, starting with 30-minute intervals to determine the optimal duration for your specific sample type. Similarly, in necessity, increase DSP concentration gradually, such as from 1 mM to 5 mM, while monitoring the solubility and sample quality. Document the outcomes of these adjustments carefully to optimize fixation conditions for your experimental needs.

Centrifuge the tube and remove DSP/Methanol from cell pellet and perform 1x wash with 1 mL methanol. Ensure thorough washing after fixation to remove excess DSP. Carefully remove and discard methanol.

Add 1 mL Methanol supplemented with 20 µL of 1 Molarity (M) Tris-HCl pH 7.5 ( 20 millimolar (mM) final) and let sit for at least 00:30:00 (larger chunks required longer times). Excess reactive DSP, being an amine-reactive crosslinker, must be quenched with Tris-HCl buffer before proceeding. The quantity must be adjusted if more fixative was used for larger tissue or higher number of cells.
Store fixed samples at -20 °C or better -80 °C for up to 4 weeks (longer is possible) or proceed immediately to rehydration.
Thaw stored samples and equilibrated to 4 °C .
Centrifuge at 850 rcf, °C, for 00:05:00 , discard supernatant.

Add 1 mL of Wash-Resuspension Buffer (0.5% BSA in 3X SSC) with (0.2-0.5 U/μL) Protector RNase Inhibitor Merck MilliporeSigma (Sigma-Aldrich) Catalog #3335402001 or RiboLock RNase Inhibitor Thermo Fisher Scientific Catalog ##EO0381
Rehydrate On ice for 00:05:00 and centrifuge at 500 rcf, °C, for rpm, °C, 5 minutes at 4 °C .

Digest using 1 mL of 200 µg/µL Liberase in PBS for 00:30:00 - 00:45:00 at 37 °C with agitation. Pipette mix every 00:15:00 . Optional: DNase I can be prepared from DNAse I Roche Catalog #04716728001 use at 1:1000 dilution. The selection of Liberase type, concentration, and digestion conditions (e.g., incubation time) must be optimized for the specific sample type being processed. This ensures effective tissue dissociation while minimizing damage to cellular or molecular targets. Always validate these parameters with pilot experiments using identical sample characteristics to refine reproducibility and performance. For more details: Liberase Research Grade Purified Enzyme Blends

Filter the cell suspension through a 70-μm cell strainer, e.g. pluriStrainer mini 70 µm (cell strainer), to remove aggregates and debris. Wash the filtered cells three times with PBS containing 1% BSA, then resuspend the washed cells in PBS with 0.04% BSA.

Filter the cell suspension through a 40-μm mesh, e.g. pluristrainer mini 40 μm (cell strainer) to remove aggregates and debris, then count the cells and adjust their concentration to 1000–1500 cells/μL for subsequent analysis.
Proceed with the chosen analysis method, ensuring sample preparation aligns with platform requirements.

Optionally, for later use or shipment, freeze cells in CryoStor CS10 100ML Fisher Scientific Catalog #NC9930384 using a controlled rate freezing container, for example Mr. Frosty Freezing Container, 2mL tubes, Nalgene Mr. Frosty Freezing Container for 1-2mL cryogenic tubes, PC, clear w/ blue lid, 1/Cs. Thermo Fisher Catalog #5100-0001
Store at -80 °C until use. Thaw samples in a 37 °C water bath and wash twice with PBS + 0.5-1% BSA.

Thoroughly equilibrate the DSP mylar package to room temperature for 00:30:00 prior to first opening. DSP (dithiobis(succinimidyl propionate)), Lomant's Reagent Thermo Fisher Catalog #22585 Critical: Only open the package after equilibration. The NHS-ester of DSP is susceptible to hydrolysis upon contact with humid atmosphere and therefore moisture-sensitive. Thorough equilibration is crucial in preventing condensation and premature inactivation of DSP.
Dissolve 50 mg DSP in 1 mL high quality DMSO, Anhydrous Thermo Fisher Catalog #D12345 for best performance. Final concentration is 50 mg/mL .

Each 10 µL aliquots can be stored in cryogenic vials with compression O-rings to prevent moisture absorption. Avoid freeze-thaw cycles by limiting the size of aliquots (up to 100 µL ). Discard any opened but unused stock.
Prior to rehydration, prepare 3X SSC by diluting 3 mL of SSC Buffer (20X) Merck MilliporeSigma (Sigma-Aldrich) Catalog #S6639 with 17 mL of Nuclease free water.
